Home / Carbon & Climate / CBAM Compliance
Carbon & Climate · Category 02

CBAM Advisory &
Reporting
for EU Exporters

The EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism taxes embedded carbon in goods exported to the EU — steel, aluminium, cement, fertilisers and electricity from 2026. Indian exporters without a compliant CBAM process face financial exposure and risk losing EU market access entirely. We calculate, report and manage CBAM compliance before it becomes a crisis.

CBAM certificates Live 2026

Fertilisers · Electricity Steel · Aluminium · Cement

Covered product categories
100%
Financial exposure without compliance
Why it matters

CBAM is the most significant
trade-linked carbon cost in history.

For Indian exporters to the EU, CBAM is not optional — it is the law. Every tonne of embedded carbon in your exported goods now carries a financial cost that grows each year with the EU carbon price.

01
Direct Financial Exposure
From January 2026, EU importers must purchase CBAM certificates for every tonne of embedded carbon in covered goods. Without an accurate CBAM calculation, your importer cannot fulfil this obligation — and you risk losing the contract.
02
Growing Enforcement Risk
The EU is systematically strengthening CBAM enforcement each year. The transition period (2023-2025) established data collection habits. From 2026, penalties for non-compliance are calibrated to the EU carbon price — potentially hundreds of thousands of euros per quarter.
03
Competitive Disadvantage
Companies with clean, auditable CBAM data become preferred suppliers for EU buyers who want to minimise their CBAM compliance burden. Companies without CBAM-ready processes become a compliance liability for their EU customers.
What we do

Complete CBAM compliance —
from calculation to quarterly reports

We manage every element of CBAM — from product-level embedded emissions calculation to quarterly CBAM transition reports and ongoing certificate monitoring.

Embedded Emissions Calculation
We calculate direct and indirect embedded emissions per product line using CBAM Regulation methodology — covering Scope 1 process emissions and Scope 2 electricity emissions per tonne of output, with the precision and documentation your EU importer needs.
Quarterly CBAM Transition Reports
During the transition period and beyond, we prepare the quarterly CBAM reports that EU importers must submit to their national authorities — using your product emission intensity data to populate the regulated report format.
Supplier Data Collection System
For complex supply chains, we design and implement a supplier carbon data collection system that captures upstream emissions data required for CBAM indirect emission calculations.
CBAM Certificate Monitoring
From 2026, we track your EU export volumes and the EU carbon price to provide real-time CBAM certificate obligation forecasts — enabling financial planning and hedging decisions.
EU Importer Support
We work directly with your EU importer’s compliance team — providing the product emission data they need in the precise format required by the EU CBAM Registry.
Our process

How we manage your CBAM compliance

A five-phase CBAM programme from product emission calculation through to ongoing certificate obligation management.

→
01
Product Scope & Boundary
Identify all CBAM-covered products in your export range, determine applicable CN codes and define the production process boundary for embedded emissions calculation.
→
02
Emissions Calculation
Calculate direct (Scope 1) and indirect (Scope 2) embedded emissions per tonne of each CBAM-covered product using CBAM Regulation Annex III methodology.
→
03
Supplier Data Collection
Design and implement data collection from upstream raw material suppliers for indirect emission inputs required by CBAM calculation methodology.
→
04
Quarterly Report Preparation
Prepare quarterly CBAM transition reports in the format required by the EU CBAM Regulation for submission by your EU importer to their national authority.
05
Certificate Obligation Management
Track EU export volumes and carbon prices to forecast annual CBAM certificate obligations and support your EU importer’s certificate procurement planning.
Key deliverables

What you receive from
every CBAM engagement

Quarterly CBAM Reports
CBAM transition period reports in EU-required format, covering all exported CBAM-covered products, ready for EU importer submission.
Product Emission Intensity Data
Verified embedded emission intensity figures (tCO2e/tonne) for each CBAM-covered product, in CBAM Regulation Annex III format.
CBAM Financial Exposure Model
Annual and quarterly CBAM certificate obligation forecasts based on export volumes and EU ETS carbon price projections — for financial planning and hedging.
Supplier Data Collection System
Structured upstream emissions data collection templates and process for indirect emission inputs required by CBAM — covering raw material and energy inputs.
Audit Trail Documentation
Complete calculation audit trail — methodology documentation, emission factors, source data and assumptions — for EU authority inspection.
EU Importer Communication Package
Formatted data submission for EU importer compliance teams, including product emission passports in CBAM Registry-compatible format.

Frameworks & Standards

CBAM within the
broader carbon compliance landscape

CBAM data feeds directly into your GHG inventory, CDP Climate response and carbon reduction strategy. We integrate these workstreams to maximise value from a single data collection effort.

EU CBAM Regulation 2023/956 CBAM Annex III Methodology GHG Protocol — Scope 1/2 ISO 14064 Emissions EU ETS Carbon Price CDP Climate Module Product Carbon Footprint ISO 14067 BRSR Climate Integration
FAQ

Frequently asked
questions

CBAM currently covers five product categories: steel and iron (including downstream products in Annex I), aluminium, cement, fertilisers (ammonia, nitric acid, urea, mixed fertilisers), and electricity. Hydrogen is also included. Each category is defined by specific CN (Combined Nomenclature) codes — we conduct a detailed product classification review to determine exactly which of your products fall within CBAM scope.

Indian exporters are not directly the reporting entity under CBAM — EU importers are. However, EU importers require accurate embedded emission data from their Indian suppliers to file the quarterly CBAM reports required by the EU CBAM Regulation. Without this data from you, your EU importer must use default emission values set by the EU, which are typically significantly higher than actual values for efficient Indian producers — increasing their CBAM certificate cost and creating financial pressure on your commercial relationship.

During the 2023-2025 transition period, EU importers must report the quantity and embedded emissions of CBAM-covered goods but are not required to purchase certificates. This is a data establishment phase. From January 2026, EU importers must surrender CBAM certificates equal to the verified embedded emissions in their imports, at a price linked to the EU ETS. The transition period data is foundational — importers who have not established accurate emission data by the time certificates are required face both compliance penalties and commercial exposure.

CBAM uses a specific methodology defined in Annex III of the CBAM Regulation. Embedded emissions include direct emissions (from fuel combustion and industrial processes within your production facility) and, for electricity-intensive products, indirect emissions (from purchased electricity). Calculations must be based on actual production data — fuel consumption, process inputs, electricity use — per tonne of output. We use primary plant data, calibrated with EU Commission emission factor tables, to produce the most accurate and commercially advantageous CBAM emission values.

Related services

Services that complement CBAM Advisory & Reporting

Carbon & Climate
GHG Inventory & Accounting
CBAM embedded emissions are a subset of your full Scope 1 and 2 GHG inventory — we build both together to eliminate duplication.

Carbon & Climate
Product Carbon Footprint
Extend CBAM embedded emission calculations into a full ISO 14067 Product Carbon Footprint for market differentiation and supply chain transparency.
Learn more →
ESG Reporting
CDP Disclosure
CBAM emission data feeds directly into CDP Climate Scope 1 and 2 responses — one dataset serves both purposes.
